title: Aurora Application Guide
strapline: A generated application reference for Aurora Galactica: audio cues by context, visual atmosphere themes, and the developer-facing presentation controls that shape the live game presentation.
currentGoal: Keep Aurora's atmosphere, sound, and presentation coherent as one application-owned system while preserving a clean boundary with Platinum, use the Galaga cue matrix to map real reference clips against Aurora states, and distinguish clearly between Aurora's own synthetic mix, the Galaga-style synthetic approximation, and the new runtime reference-audio mode that plays actual extracted Galaga clips.

audioContexts.label: Navigation Tick
audioContexts.listenFor: Light Aurora shell tick, not gameplay urgency.
audioContexts.description: Subtle front-door navigation tick for Aurora-owned shell identity surfaces.

audioContexts.label: Confirm
audioContexts.listenFor: Short confirm flourish with a polished shell feel.
audioContexts.description: Confirmation cue for front-door and shell-level Aurora selections.

audioContexts.label: Score Rotation Enter
audioContexts.listenFor: A small rising motif that feels like the board is waking up.
audioContexts.description: Entry sting when Aurora transitions into the score-focused wait-mode surface.

audioContexts.label: Score Rotation Pulse
audioContexts.listenFor: Gentle repeating pulse that stays ambient rather than mechanical.
audioContexts.description: Recurring pulse that keeps the score rotation alive without sounding like gameplay.

audioContexts.label: Demo Entry
audioContexts.listenFor: Aurora identity at the handoff from shell to autoplay.
audioContexts.description: Bridge from the Aurora shell identity into autoplay demo mode.

audioContexts.label: Demo Pulse
audioContexts.listenFor: Classic arcade cadence once motion on the board begins.
audioContexts.description: Ongoing demo pulse that leans back toward the classic arcade cadence once play is underway.

audioContexts.label: Stage 1 Start
audioContexts.listenFor: A crisp arcade rise that feels close to classic stage-start energy.
audioContexts.description: Opening cue for classic early-stage Aurora presentation.

audioContexts.label: Aurora Surge Start
audioContexts.listenFor: A brighter upward flourish that feels more expansive than the classic set.
audioContexts.description: Opening cue for the later Aurora surge/borealis stage family.

audioContexts.label: Formation Pulse Classic
audioContexts.listenFor: Clear march-like pulse that supports classic board pressure.
audioContexts.description: Formation march/pulse for classic early-stage cadence.

audioContexts.label: Formation Pulse Aurora
audioContexts.listenFor: A more glassy pulse that still reads as rhythm, not ambience.
audioContexts.description: Formation pulse for the Aurora-themed later-stage family.

audioContexts.label: Player Shot Classic
audioContexts.description: Early-stage player shot reference aligned to the classic arcade mix.

audioContexts.label: Player Shot Aurora
audioContexts.description: Later-stage Aurora shot variant with a slightly sleeker synth edge.

audioContexts.label: Enemy Shot Classic
audioContexts.description: Enemy projectile cue for classic stages.

audioContexts.label: Enemy Shot Aurora
audioContexts.description: Enemy projectile cue for Aurora-leaning later stages.

audioContexts.label: Enemy Hit
audioContexts.description: Standard enemy hit cue used across Aurora combat.

audioContexts.label: Boss Hit
audioContexts.description: Heavier impact cue for boss and stronger target hits.

audioContexts.label: Enemy Boom
audioContexts.description: Standard enemy destruction cue.

audioContexts.label: Boss Boom
audioContexts.description: Larger destruction cue for boss enemies and rogue fighter losses.

audioContexts.label: Capture Beam
audioContexts.description: Beam/deploy cue for the capture sequence.

audioContexts.label: Capture Retreat
audioContexts.description: Recovery cue when the capture sequence resolves and retreats.

audioContexts.label: Fighter Captured
audioContexts.listenFor: A clear loss-state handoff before the boss retreats with the captured ship.
audioContexts.description: Distinct cue for the exact moment the player's fighter is fully captured.

audioContexts.label: Captured Fighter Destroyed
audioContexts.listenFor: A sharp, regrettable destruction cue distinct from ordinary enemy hits.
audioContexts.description: Cue used when the carried/captured fighter itself is destroyed for points.

audioContexts.label: Rescue Join
audioContexts.description: Join cue for dual-fighter reunification and rescue success.

audioContexts.label: Bonus Ship Award
audioContexts.description: Extra-ship award cue used when Aurora score extends trigger.

audioContexts.label: Ship Loss
audioContexts.description: Player ship destruction cue during live play.

audioContexts.label: Stage Transition
audioContexts.listenFor: A short rising handoff with more melodic identity than a simple beep.
audioContexts.description: Transition cue between standard stages.

audioContexts.label: Challenge Transition
audioContexts.listenFor: A distinct bonus-stage announcement with more lift and ceremony.
audioContexts.description: Transition cue into challenge/bonus stage presentation.

audioContexts.label: Challenge Results
audioContexts.listenFor: A clean wrap-up phrase that feels like challenge scoring has concluded.
audioContexts.description: Result cue for challenge-stage completion when the run was not perfect.

audioContexts.label: Challenge Perfect
audioContexts.listenFor: A more celebratory result than the standard challenge wrap-up.
audioContexts.description: Distinct challenge-stage result cue for perfect clears.

audioContexts.label: Game Over
audioContexts.listenFor: A readable downward ending phrase, not just an impact effect.
audioContexts.description: End-of-run cue used by Aurora results/game-over flow.

audioContexts.label: High Score 1st
audioContexts.listenFor: A stronger celebratory placement cue for first place entry.
audioContexts.description: Rank-specific scoreboard cue when a run enters first place.

audioContexts.label: High Score 2nd-10th
audioContexts.listenFor: A positive but smaller placement cue for non-first leaderboard entries.
audioContexts.description: Rank-specific scoreboard cue when a run enters the board outside first place.

audioContexts.label: Dive Charge / Falling Alien
audioContexts.listenFor: The charge cue should begin with the enemy dive, not with bullet fire.
audioContexts.description: Cue used when an enemy commits to a dive or attack run toward the player.

audioContexts.label: Formation Arrival Signal
audioContexts.listenFor: Short interstitial signal after start music and before alien arrival begins.
audioContexts.description: Pre-entry signal used to separate the opening start phrase from the first enemy formation arrival in Galaga reference mode.

comparisonSets.label: Demo Pulse
comparisonSets.focus: Compare autoplay cadence and whether the board feels mechanically alive enough once demo motion begins.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture; original 4.148s window plus 0.450s-0.690s candidate

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Synthetic Galaga runtime cue vs actual indexed reference clip.

comparisonSets.label: Stage Start
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether stage start feels like a clear arcade start/announcement instead of a generic cue.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Best current stage-start mapping from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: Formation Pulse
comparisonSets.focus: Compare ongoing formation cadence and whether gameplay feels like it has enough marching pressure.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Strongest current reference for the board cadence bed.

comparisonSets.label: Player Shot
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether player fire reads as a quick arcade shot instead of a generic impact.

referenceWindow.selection: distinct initial high-transient subwindow reserved for player fire; lower score than the shared best window but prevents playerShot/enemyShot/bossHit semantic collapse

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.05-0.29s flagship/fighter-shot window; player fire is a single crisp transient rather than a multi-phase phrase

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured subwindow promoted for shot identity; uses a distinct initial transient so player fire can be scored separately from enemy fire and boss damage.

comparisonSets.label: Enemy Shot
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether enemy fire is distinct enough from player fire and boss damage.

referenceWindow.selection: second measured high-transient subwindow reserved for enemy projectile feedback so enemyShot no longer scores against the same active phrase as playerShot

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.90-1.14s flagship/fighter-shot window; enemy fire is a single projectile transient

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured subwindow promoted for enemy projectile feedback; still from the same source clip, but scored as a distinct reference phrase from playerShot and bossHit.

comparisonSets.label: Enemy Hit
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether an enemy impact gives immediate hit confirmation.

referenceWindow.selection: later Zako phrase reserved for immediate hit confirmation, separated from the normal-enemy destruction subwindow

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.75-0.99s Zako window; enemy hit is scored as immediate damage confirmation

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured subwindow promoted for impact feedback; hit and kill hierarchy remains under review but is now scored against a distinct phrase from enemyBoom.

comparisonSets.label: Boss Hit
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether boss damage reads as a distinct multi-hit event.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring measured subwindow for boss damage confirmation, reserved for multi-hit boss feedback rather than generic shot identity

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 1.149-1.439s boss-damage window; boss hit is a distinct damage confirmation transient

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured subwindow promoted for boss damage feedback; reference source remains shared, but bossHit now has a dedicated scoring window.

comparisonSets.label: Enemy Boom
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether normal enemy destruction is more emphatic than a simple hit.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring measured Zako subwindow reserved for normal-enemy destruction, separated from enemyHit

referenceSegmentation.source: curated from aurora-audio-theme-comparison/2026-05-09-main-6c0e2b61 measured Zako destruction transient
referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.50-0.74s Zako window; normal enemy destruction is a compact finality transient

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured subwindow promoted for normal-enemy destruction feedback; enemyHit and enemyBoom now score against separate Zako phrases.

comparisonSets.label: Boss Boom
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether boss destruction feels larger and more final than normal enemy destruction.

referenceWindow.selection: full active boss-death phrase so destruction can be scored as a larger multi-burst event rather than a single generic explosion

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.798-1.567s boss-death window; labels early, middle, and final destruction bursts

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured coverage added for boss destruction feedback.

comparisonSets.label: Capture Beam
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether the beam deploy moment reads clearly as tractor-beam danger.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

referenceSegmentation.source: curated from measured tractor-beam reference window after repeatability-gate pass
referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 3.199-3.679s tractor-beam window; labels separate beam strike from sustained beam body

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Strong direct mapping.

comparisonSets.label: Capture Retreat
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether the post-capture retreat phase remains audible as a distinct state.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Measured coverage added for the transition between beam danger and captured-fighter state.

comparisonSets.label: Fighter Captured
comparisonSets.focus: Compare the exact moment the fighter is fully captured, distinct from beam deploy and retreat.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: Captured Fighter Destroyed
comparisonSets.focus: Compare the regret/penalty moment when the carried fighter is destroyed.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 1.949-2.189s captured-fighter-destroyed window; penalty feedback is a compact regret/destruction transient

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: Rescue Join
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether the rescue/join moment feels celebratory and distinct from ordinary scoring.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring stable keeper after focused rescueJoin candidate loop and full-theme promotion precheck

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Strong direct mapping.

comparisonSets.label: Ship Loss
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether ship loss feels like a real Galaga death moment rather than a generic impact.

referenceSegmentation.source: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail

analysisPolicy.reason: Ship loss is a composite onset/body/tail event; conformance should score the scheduled cue window rather than the single loudest active island.

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Strong direct mapping.

comparisonSets.label: Challenge Transition
comparisonSets.focus: Compare how distinct and ceremonial the challenge-stage announcement feels.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Strong direct mapping.

comparisonSets.label: Challenge Results
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether challenge wrap-up feels like a proper result phrase instead of a generic transition.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

referenceSegmentation.source: curated from challenging-stage-results reference window after challengeResults event-gap pass
referenceSegmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.75-1.17s challenge-results phrase; labels distinguish attack, result body, and falloff

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: Challenge Perfect
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether a perfect challenge result feels more celebratory than the standard results cue.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: High Score 1st
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether first place entry feels distinct and more important than a lower board placement.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: High Score 2nd-10th
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether non-first leaderboard entry feels positive but smaller than first place.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: New Aurora cue slot added from the cue matrix.

comparisonSets.label: Game Over
comparisonSets.focus: Compare whether the ending cue feels final and emotionally complete enough.

referenceWindow.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

comparisonSets.mappingStatus: Best current end-state reference; still broader than Aurora's single slot.

graphicsThemes.label: Classic Arcade
graphicsThemes.backgrounds: frontDoor classic-stars · wait classic-stars · demo classic-stars · stage classic-stars · challenge classic-stars
graphicsThemes.description: The closest Aurora gets to the original Galaga look: bright, readable stars on a black field with classic cadence and no aurora ribbon wash.

graphicsThemes.label: Aurora Hint
graphicsThemes.backgrounds: frontDoor aurora-borealis · wait aurora-borealis · demo aurora-hint · stage aurora-hint · challenge aurora-hint
graphicsThemes.description: Bridge theme where the classic field remains visible but the Aurora wash starts to become part of the stage identity.

graphicsThemes.label: Aurora Borealis
graphicsThemes.backgrounds: frontDoor aurora-borealis · wait aurora-borealis · demo aurora-borealis · stage aurora-borealis · challenge aurora-borealis
graphicsThemes.description: Full Aurora stage theme with softer stars under a stronger ribbon treatment and the main later-stage audio palette.

graphicsThemes.label: Aurora Crown
graphicsThemes.backgrounds: frontDoor aurora-borealis · wait aurora-borealis · demo aurora-borealis · stage aurora-borealis · challenge aurora-borealis
graphicsThemes.description: Aurora-owned shell/front-door theme used to establish the application identity before gameplay falls back toward classic or surge contexts.

graphicsContexts.label: Front Door Atmosphere
graphicsContexts.description: The front door keeps Aurora-owned identity with a fuller ribbon field and crown audio theme while still living inside the Platinum shell.

graphicsContexts.label: Wait Mode Surface
graphicsContexts.description: Score rotation/wait mode shares the Aurora front-door family so the shell remains coherent between idle states.

graphicsContexts.label: Stage 1 Classic Gameplay
graphicsContexts.description: Stage 1 keeps the classic stars look as the gameplay baseline, with the brighter starfield tuned against Galaga references.

graphicsContexts.label: Stage 8 Aurora Surge
graphicsContexts.description: Later stages shift into the stronger Aurora visual family with softer star density and ribbon emphasis.

graphicsContexts.label: Challenge / Bonus Stage
graphicsContexts.description: Challenge stages keep the configured Aurora background family rather than reverting to a special hardcoded board.

shipCatalog.name: Player Fighter
shipCatalog.appears: Every standard and challenge stage as the main player ship.
shipCatalog.context: Primary player-controlled ship. Can be captured, rescued, and rejoined into dual-fighter mode.
shipCatalog.notes: The visual baseline for reserve pips, ship loss, rescue, and extra-ship award feedback.

shipCatalog.name: Dual Fighter
shipCatalog.appears: After a successful rescue join during normal Aurora play.
shipCatalog.context: A paired player state rather than a separate enemy or pack. Central to Aurora's rescue and trust-fix behavior.
shipCatalog.notes: Should be documented separately because it changes shot spread, survival logic, and recovery expectations.

shipCatalog.name: Bee Line
shipCatalog.appears: Lower-pressure enemy line across standard stages, with family reskins tied to stage bands.
shipCatalog.context: Fast, lighter-value formation and dive enemy. Forms part of the classic opening pressure and remains present through later bands.
shipCatalog.notes: Uses `type: bee` in gameplay state and changes family art with stage-band progression.

shipCatalog.name: Butterfly Line
shipCatalog.appears: Main non-boss formation rows across standard stages.
shipCatalog.context: Mid-tier enemy line with higher scoring and more visible board presence than bees.
shipCatalog.notes: Uses `type: but` in gameplay state and shares family progression with the stage band.

shipCatalog.name: Boss / Super Boss
shipCatalog.appears: Top formation row in standard stages, with later themed boss families and archetypes.
shipCatalog.context: Higher-health enemy that can dive solo or with escorts and is central to capture/rescue flow.
shipCatalog.notes: Uses `type: boss`, starts with extra health, and picks up named archetypes like `command-core`, `super-boss-scorpion`, `partner-wing-stingray`, `council-boss`, and `super-partner-pair` through stage presentation.

shipCatalog.name: Rogue Fighter
shipCatalog.appears: When a captured fighter is converted into an enemy state.
shipCatalog.context: High-value special case enemy tied directly to the capture lifecycle.
shipCatalog.notes: Uses `type: rogue` and its own dedicated family/visual treatment rather than the standard band families.

shipCatalog.name: Challenge Dragonfly Family
shipCatalog.appears: Challenge stages from stage 11 onward until the mosquito family takes over.
shipCatalog.context: Challenge-only presentation family used to make bonus-stage waves feel distinct from the normal rack.
shipCatalog.notes: Not a separate gameplay `type`; it is a challenge-family skin applied to challenge enemies.

shipCatalog.name: Challenge Mosquito Family
shipCatalog.appears: Late challenge stages from stage 19 onward.
shipCatalog.context: Late challenge-family visual used for stronger bonus-stage identity deeper into the Aurora progression.
shipCatalog.notes: Like dragonfly, this is a challenge-family presentation layer rather than a new base gameplay type.

stageFamilies.families: bee classic · but classic · boss classic · challenge classic
stageFamilies.description: Classic opening Aurora band. Closest to original Galaga feel in stars, enemy look, and cadence.

stageFamilies.families: bee scorpion · but scorpion · boss classic · challenge classic
stageFamilies.description: First Aurora-flavored band. Adds scorpion family visuals while keeping the challenge family conservative.

stageFamilies.families: bee stingray · but stingray · boss stingray · challenge dragonfly
stageFamilies.description: Main later-stage Aurora band where both background and audio identity shift clearly away from the classic opening.

stageFamilies.families: bee galboss · but galboss · boss galboss · challenge dragonfly
stageFamilies.description: Heavier late-stage band with the galboss family and stronger aurora presentation.

stageFamilies.families: bee galboss · but galboss · boss galboss · challenge mosquito
stageFamilies.description: Late Aurora crown band tying the strongest shell/front-door identity back into the deeper stage family.

conformanceAlienRows.name: Bee / Zako Scout
conformanceAlienRows.runtime: type bee; families classic, scorpion, stingray, galboss
conformanceAlienRows.activity: Basic formation alien; dives, fires, supplies early pressure, and appears in challenge groups.
conformanceAlienRows.presence: All regular stages and challenge stages.
conformanceAlienRows.reference: Galaga Zako/scout behavior, challenge-stage evidence, and galaga3-zako audio reference.
conformanceAlienRows.confidence: medium; visual likeness is still low-confidence proxy
conformanceAlienRows.next: Score Aurora displayed sprites against the inferred Galaga consensus models and source-frame pixel targets, then promote lossless/emulator targets when available.

conformanceAlienRows.name: Butterfly / Escort Alien
conformanceAlienRows.runtime: type but; families classic, scorpion, stingray, galboss
conformanceAlienRows.activity: Mid-value formation alien; dives more dangerously, escorts bosses in special attacks, and participates in challenge set pieces.
conformanceAlienRows.presence: All regular stages, with high-value role in stages 4 and later.
conformanceAlienRows.reference: Galaga butterfly/escort behavior through boss/formation grammar and path-family reports.
conformanceAlienRows.next: Run per-role visual scoring against the inferred Galaga consensus model and separate escort-path precision against reference windows.

conformanceAlienRows.name: Boss Galaga / Command Boss
conformanceAlienRows.runtime: type boss; families classic, stingray, galboss
conformanceAlienRows.activity: Two-hit boss; captures player ship, carries captured fighter, leads special attacks, and awards high dive/squadron points.
conformanceAlienRows.presence: All regular stages; capture/rescue and special-attack windows.
conformanceAlienRows.reference: Galaga boss, tractor beam, capture/rescue, boss-hit, and boss-death timing/audio references.
conformanceAlienRows.confidence: medium-high for behavior; audiovisual damage feedback still weaker
conformanceAlienRows.next: Improve explosion/damage visual event authenticity and direct boss path precision from extracted reference paths.

conformanceAlienRows.name: Challenge Rogue / Specialty Alien
conformanceAlienRows.runtime: type rogue in challenge and captured-fighter contexts
conformanceAlienRows.activity: Challenge-stage novelty role used to create distinct set pieces and visible learning opportunities.
conformanceAlienRows.presence: Challenge stages 7, 11, and 15 in current evidence windows.
conformanceAlienRows.reference: Galaga challenging-stage specialty waves and bonus-stage composition.
conformanceAlienRows.next: Promote a challenge-only Galaga target when the ingestion has a clean bonus-stage sheet, then score reward clarity separately.

conformanceAlienRows.name: Captured Fighter
conformanceAlienRows.runtime: carried player fighter state on boss
conformanceAlienRows.activity: Converts player state into risk/reward: destroy too early for loss, rescue for dual fighter.
conformanceAlienRows.reference: Galaga capture/rescue audio and event flow correspondence artifacts.
conformanceAlienRows.confidence: medium-high for state flow; visual feedback lower
conformanceAlienRows.next: Use the generated dual-fighter target for rescue/carry review, then add visual event scoring for destruction, rescue join, and player comprehension.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Begins run and anchors formation-arrival timing.
conformanceAudioRows.next: Continue waveform/spectral comparison and fit full reference phrases where runtime windows allow.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Differentiates player action from enemy pressure.
conformanceAudioRows.next: Improve cue distinctness, cooldown fit, and event alignment without masking hit feedback.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Immediate feedback that a missile impacted or destroyed a normal alien.
conformanceAudioRows.reference: galaga3-zako subwindows and aurora-audio-event-gap latest report.
conformanceAudioRows.next: Add event-specific explosion/impact scorer for missile impact, alien death, ship impact, immunity, and boss damage.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Shows multi-hit boss damage and final destruction clearly.
conformanceAudioRows.score: measured but still in audio gap
conformanceAudioRows.next: Score first-hit flash, sound onset, hold/cadence, final explosion, and player comprehension.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Explains the most important Galaga-style risk/reward loop.
conformanceAudioRows.reference: Tractor beam, captured-fighter, capturing, rescued-double-ship, and captured-fighter-destroyed clips.
conformanceAudioRows.score: flow guardrail pass; rescueJoin keeper promoted after full-theme precheck
conformanceAudioRows.next: Improve event-rich logs and status/commentary hooks around capture and rescue, then revisit captureBeam tail separation.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Explains ship loss, final loss, and the emotional end of a run without muting the next start.
conformanceAudioRows.reference: galaga3-death and game-over reference windows with onset/body/tail segmentation.
conformanceAudioRows.score: measured ship-loss cue promoted; residual playerHit tail is still the highest current segment gap
conformanceAudioRows.next: Refine the remaining tail/body fit with composite modeling and browser-capture calibration before attempting another runtime audio promotion.

conformanceAudioRows.meaning: Marks bonus-stage entry and result/reward feedback.
conformanceAudioRows.reference: galaga challenging-stage, results, and perfect-result clips.
conformanceAudioRows.next: Add score/result surface conformance and direct challenge-stage audio fit checks.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Baseline rack entry, first dives, low pressure, player onboarding.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: Formation arrival timing, first dive timing, safe early pressure, classic visual/audio framing.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: stage-1-baseline evidence window; stage-1 geometry and shot trust guardrails.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Improve direct visual reference grounding for alien/player likeness.

stageConformanceRows.summary: First challenging stage and bonus-stage learning moment. Current critical read: the rule baseline is present, but the set piece does not yet read enough like Galaga's first challenge.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: Non-lethal challenge behavior, no enemy shooting, no ship loss, visible arrival and peel-off patterns, bee/butterfly bonus-line grammar.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: Challenge timing guardrails, challenge-stage candidate window, and reference-artifacts/analyses/challenge-stage-conformance/latest.json.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Make stage 3 best-match challenge-1 arrival/late-wave references instead of a later mixed challenge target.

stageConformanceRows.summary: First major pressure step and capture/squadron importance.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: Boss capture, special attacks, lane pressure, player loss windows, fairness.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: Stage 4 pressure geometry, lane loss windows, close-shot trust harnesses.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Continue pressure precision while protecting fairness and persona safety.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Scorpion cross-sweep challenge set piece. Current critical read: the label variation exists, but the measured vector still clusters too close to the same broad challenge-reference family.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: No enemy shooting, no ship loss, mixed alien families, cross-sweep path family, higher-density bonus pattern.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: challenge-stage-scorpion-cross, alien-entry/challenge-variation, formation-boss path extraction, and challenge-stage conformance artifacts.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Turn stage 7 into a true challenge-2 contract with group-by-group mixed-novelty scoring and visible entry/exit differences.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Stingray hook-arc challenge set piece. Current critical read: dragonfly family presentation is visible, but active sprite-motion novelty is not yet measured.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: No enemy shooting, no ship loss, new challenge-family visual language, hook-arc motion, later-stage bonus pattern novelty.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: challenge-stage-stingray-hook, path-family comparison, and challenge-stage conformance artifact.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Add challenge-3 reference phase scoring and active visual-motion scoring for flapping, pulsing, and silhouette changes.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Late-run cleanup, pressure, squadron reward, and advanced route planning.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: Higher pressure, special-attack scoring, escort recovery route, advanced-persona outcomes.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: late-run-cleanup-or-failure, late-run-escort-variant, level-arc probes, escort-reward sweeps.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Improve direct late-stage path/cadence precision and replay-style pressure analysis.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Boss-led late-loop challenge set piece. Current critical read: the widest motion shape is present, but it still lacks a mature late-challenge reference target.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: No enemy shooting, no ship loss, boss-heavy formation choreography, late-loop motion, advanced bonus-stage path reading.
stageConformanceRows.evidence: challenge-stage-boss-led-loop, formation-boss path-slot extraction, alien-entry challenge variation, and challenge-stage conformance artifact.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Capture or label later Galaga challenge references, then tune boss-led late-loop exits and high-bonus readability against that target.

stageConformanceRows.summary: Crown split-cascade challenge set piece configured for very-late play, but not yet mature as a documented conformance target.
stageConformanceRows.aspects: No enemy shooting, no ship loss, mosquito-family visual novelty, split/cascade path identity, late-stage bonus complexity.
stageConformanceRows.gap: Create durable stage-19 evidence windows and late Galaga challenge reference labels before treating this as conformant.

personaRows.label: Beginner
personaRows.expected: Moves less efficiently, misses more challenge opportunities, but should still experience readable cause and effect.
personaRows.checks: Stage 1, Stage 2 safety, first challenge clarity.

personaRows.label: Intermediate
personaRows.expected: Clears early waves more often and engages capture/rescue and challenge bonuses inconsistently.
personaRows.checks: Mid-run pressure, challenge hit rate, Stage 4 fairness.

personaRows.label: Expert
personaRows.expected: Reaches deeper stage bands, positions better, and reveals whether reward routes are viable.

personaRows.label: Professional
personaRows.role: High-skill regression guard and release-confidence proxy.
personaRows.expected: Exploits strong routes without collapsing from unfair timing or broken scoring.
personaRows.checks: Professional stage-2 checks, long-run progression ordering, late reward windows.

graphicsControls.label: Audio Theme
graphicsControls.description: Developer override for phase-resolved audio routing. `aurora-application` uses Aurora's current synthetic application mix. `galaga-original-reference` uses the synthetic classic-leaning approximation. `galaga-reference-assets` plays the extracted Galaga reference clips for the mapped gameplay/challenge/results moments.

graphicsControls.label: Graphics Theme
graphicsControls.description: Developer override for the visual atmosphere family. It changes rendered backgrounds and starfield profiles without changing the resolved audio theme.

graphicsControls.label: Starfield Intensity
graphicsControls.description: Multiplier applied to star brightness so we can tune classic visibility against reference material without rewriting the base theme.

graphicsControls.label: Starfield Speed
graphicsControls.description: Multiplier applied to live starfield speed so we can compare Aurora against classic Galaga movement references.

links.label: Hosted Project Guide
links.detail: Overall project, release, and platform/application boundary guide.

links.detail: Platform-owned structure, responsibilities, and release lane model.

links.label: Hosted Player Guide
links.detail: Current user-facing play and controls guide.

links.label: Galaga Reference Baseline
links.detail: Catalog of visual, motion, and audio references used for Aurora tuning, including stage-play stills and the labeled Galaga sound reference video.

links.label: Galaga Audio Cue Matrix
links.detail: Canonical mapping from Aurora cue slots to the strongest current Galaga reference clips, including missing game-owned slots.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on menu move / focus change.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by shell navigation events so the shell feels responsive before gameplay starts.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. No explicit stop rule.
audioEventMatrix.note: Application-owned shell cue, not part of Galaga reference timing.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on confirm / activation.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by shell confirm actions such as opening panels or starting a selection.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. No explicit stop rule.
audioEventMatrix.note: Application-owned shell cue.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Should occur before game start when a credit or start-ready action is accepted.
audioEventMatrix.audioName: Missing runtime slot; reference available: galaga3-coin-inserted.m4a
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Not currently modeled in Aurora. Would likely belong at the shell-to-start boundary if we add a credit/start-ready state.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Would be a one-shot cue with no stop rule.
audioEventMatrix.note: Missing event slot. Reference clip exists and is a good future fidelity target.

audioEventMatrix.timing: On entering wait mode from the shell or after attract/demo reset.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when Aurora enters the score-focused wait surface.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. Replaced naturally by later pulses.
audioEventMatrix.note: Aurora-owned shell/wait identity cue.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Every 2.6s while wait-mode score rotation is active.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by the wait-mode pulse timer to keep the surface alive without entering gameplay cadence.
audioEventMatrix.stop: No explicit stop; naturally suppressed when phase changes out of wait mode.
audioEventMatrix.note: Ambient shell pulse, not intended to match Galaga gameplay timing.

audioEventMatrix.timing: At the handoff from wait/front door into autoplay demo.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated once the attract loop leaves score rotation and starts demo play.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. Followed by demo pulse cadence.
audioEventMatrix.note: Bridge from Aurora shell into board motion.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Every 2.15s while demo is active.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by the attract demo pulse timer.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Ends when demo ends or a different phase takes over.
audioEventMatrix.note: In Galaga reference mode this uses the convoy ambience clip rather than a shell pulse.

audioEventMatrix.timing: In Galaga Reference Audio mode, gameStart now begins immediately on start input and preserves the full 4.0s reference phrase. The opening board cadence stays held until the later formation-arrival signal and first enemy entry window.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated immediately after pressing Start for the measured stage-1 opening flow in Galaga reference mode.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops attractEnter, attractPulse, and stagePulse before playing. One-shot cue.
audioEventMatrix.note: Measured-reference policy: preserve the canonical 4.0s phrase first, then place the arrival signal and first visible enemies after it.

audioEventMatrix.timing: In Galaga Reference Audio mode on stage 1, it lands about 4.18s after start input, after the full game-start phrase and before the first visible enemy arrivals around 5.35s.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by a dedicated formation-arrival timer during the stage 1 opening handoff.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops stagePulse before playback. One-shot cue.
audioEventMatrix.note: This is the interstitial 'they are coming' beat between the preserved opening phrase and the first visible arrivals.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Gameplay requests a pulse roughly every 0.38s, but Galaga reference mode throttles actual clip playback to about every 1.25s and suppresses it during hold windows. In the measured stage-1 opening it stays held until after the first-arrival window, around 6.15s from start input, and later stage entries now use widened reference timing windows before cadence resumes.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by the ongoing gameplay cadence loop once the stage is active and no transition/result hold is in force.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stopped or held during stage transitions, challenge transitions, and challenge result windows.
audioEventMatrix.note: This still needs continued timing review against visible movement, but the opening window is now driven by measured reference timing rather than a convenience-shortened handoff.

audioEventMatrix.timing: In Galaga Reference Audio mode, the cue is intentionally delayed about 0.12s after dive start and after visible downward travel begins, instead of firing on the exact trigger frame.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated from enemy dive start, including scripted and random dives.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. No explicit stop rule.
audioEventMatrix.note: Current known issue area: timing against visible dive movement still needs tuning.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on player fire.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated by the player firing a shot.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. Cooldown-limited.
audioEventMatrix.note: Fidelity gap: Galaga reference mode currently shares this clip with enemyShot and bossHit, so the event identity is too collapsed.

audioEventMatrix.timing: When an enemy projectile is fired, not when a dive starts.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated from enemy bullet fire during formation or dive attacks.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue. Cooldown-limited.
audioEventMatrix.note: Likely too broad: it currently reuses the same reference clip as playerShot and bossHit.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on successful hit / kill confirmation.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated on enemy damage or kill resolution.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cues. Cooldown-limited.
audioEventMatrix.note: Reference mode currently uses the same Zako clip for both hit and boom, so destruction hierarchy is still compressed.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on boss damage or kill.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated on boss damage and boss destruction events.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cues. Cooldown-limited.
audioEventMatrix.note: Boss death has a dedicated clip. Boss first-hit now also gets a dedicated visual flash/emphasis pass, but bossHit still shares the mixed damage/shot clip and may need a cleaner mapping.
audioEventMatrix.status: Implemented, visual emphasis improved and mapping still under review

audioEventMatrix.timing: When the boss enters beam state during a capture attempt.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when a capture boss reaches the beam phase and begins the beam attack.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown. Beam state itself ends separately.
audioEventMatrix.note: Good direct reference mapping.

audioEventMatrix.timing: At the exact moment the player fighter is fully captured.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when the capture sequence completes successfully.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Important trust/fidelity event and now a dedicated Aurora-owned slot.

audioEventMatrix.event: Capture retreat / boss exits with fighter
audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediately after a successful capture as the boss retreats with the fighter.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated after capture success when the boss leaves with the captured ship.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Mapping is plausible but still semantically fuzzy; may need a better dedicated reference choice.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate when the carried fighter is destroyed.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when the carried fighter itself is hit and destroyed.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Dedicated slot and direct reference mapping.

audioEventMatrix.timing: When the released fighter docks back and the dual-fighter state is established.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated at the rescue-join completion beat.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Dedicated slot and measured reference-window keeper promoted after full-theme precheck.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate when the extend threshold is crossed.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated from score-threshold logic when an extra ship is awarded.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Direct reference mapping.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Immediate on ship loss.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated directly from the ship-loss event before respawn or game-over flow continues.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Audio fires on the loss event, but visual/audio sync still needs review.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Queued late in the inter-stage window. The runtime cue now preserves a 3.35s reference-backed phrase when the handoff budget allows, so the inter-level moment no longer collapses into a short flag blip.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated during the inter-stage handoff once the board has cleared and the transition timer reaches its cue point.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops stagePulse, challengeResults, and challengePerfect before playback.
audioEventMatrix.note: Now cue-budgeted and backed by reference timing windows, with later-stage entry delays used to keep arrival/cadence from stepping on the handoff.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Queued late in the normal-to-challenge window. The challenge-entry window is now 3.35s; the cue starts about 1.13s before challenge spawn, uses a 1.6s excerpt, and preserves about 0.47s of cue tail past spawn in reference mode.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when the next stage is a challenge stage and the transition timer reaches the challenge cue point.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops stagePulse, stageTransition, challengeResults, and challengePerfect before playback.
audioEventMatrix.note: Still one of the most sensitive timing/problem areas in live play, but it is now managed by a named reference timing window instead of a single hardcoded delay.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Queued about 0.34s after challenge clear; the post-challenge result hold is now 4.25s and the next-stage handoff window is 7.35s, giving the full 4.0s reference phrase room to finish before the next-stage cue.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when a challenge stage is cleared without a perfect result.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops stagePulse, stageTransition, and challengeTransition before playback; also holds ambient cadence.
audioEventMatrix.note: Now separated from challengePerfect and given a wider hold window so the result phrase can finish before the next-stage handoff.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Queued about 0.34s after a perfect challenge clear; excerpt runs the full 4.0s reference phrase with the same widened result hold and next-stage handoff window as challenge results.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when a challenge stage is cleared perfectly.
audioEventMatrix.stop: Stops stagePulse, stageTransition, and challengeTransition before playback; also holds ambient cadence.
audioEventMatrix.note: Direct reference mapping with runtime-safe duration.

audioEventMatrix.timing: On terminal ship loss when the game-over state is entered.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated from the gameOver transition after the final ship is lost.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Uses the long ambience reference; may later need a dedicated runtime-safe excerpt if it collides with results flow.

audioEventMatrix.timing: When game-over/results enters a 1st-place name-entry flow.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when the results rank is exactly first place.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Dedicated slot.

audioEventMatrix.timing: When game-over/results enters a non-first high-score entry flow.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Initiated when the results rank is a non-first leaderboard placement.
audioEventMatrix.stop: One-shot cue with cooldown.
audioEventMatrix.note: Currently uses the 2nd-5th reference clip for the broader non-first ranking bucket.

audioEventMatrix.timing: Would occur on specific enemy-family attack or kill moments.
audioEventMatrix.trigger: Not currently modeled as distinct Aurora cue slots; Aurora collapses these into generic attackCharge, enemyHit, enemyBoom, and bossHit cues.
audioEventMatrix.stop: N/A
audioEventMatrix.note: Likely future fidelity feature rather than a current bug, but it explains why some combat events still feel flattened compared to Galaga.
